stance: Risk-on within a diversified mandate; lean the book toward names with rising analyst revisions, prune the refuted low-revision legacy positions, and avoid thin-data momentum/value mirages.
experience_note: last_round_review is null, so I have nothing graded yet — no hit-rate, no conviction-IC. I am pre-registering this as the baseline round. What I am watching going forward: (1) do my strong-revision adds (ONGC, NMDC, Muthoot, LICI, Gland, Coforge, Grasim) out-earn my falling-revision exits (Siemens, ABB, RBL, Timken, Fortis) over the next 1-3 months — that is the direct test of whether ARM tilting works on this desk; (2) whether sizing winners bigger (conviction-IC) actually pays, since I deliberately kept my spread tight this round; (3) whether the flat momentum/value cross-section was real or a stale-data round — if z_mom/z_val come alive next round I must re-weight them in. What would change my mind: if the next review shows my high-ARM adds underperformed my low-ARM exits, I will cut the ARM tilt's size, because that is the signal doing the work this round and its IC is small by design.
